user_query:
I need a local mock interview prep workflow that keeps memory of my preferences and decisions and automatically recalls them. Please do all of the following and keep everything in this workspace:

1) Read input/conversation.jsonl (multi-turn transcript) and extract structured facts (preferences, decisions, people, action items). For each fact, include content, category, visibility (shared/private/secret), confidence, occurrence_count, and timestamps. If any updates are implied, include previous_value or supersedes. Write the array to output/memento/facts.json.

2) Using those facts, write a recall summary tailored for my next practice session to output/memento/recall.md. Do not include any secret fact content in the recall. Include a line exactly like: "Secret facts excluded: <number>" where <number> is the count of facts in facts.json whose visibility is secret. If you can infer any causal relations, mention them (e.g., caused_by or precondition_of) in the summary.

3) Read input/sample_user_messages.txt. Create a config that prepends a persistent context to every user message (e.g., "Use memory before answering; apply interview guidelines; keep answers concise.") and save JSON to output/config/injection.json with fields: { enabled: true, prependText: "..." }. Also generate output/injected/messages.txt where each line from input/sample_user_messages.txt is transformed to start with the exact prependText from the config.

4) Run a short mock interview based on the target role described in input/JD.txt and my profile in input/profile.json. Produce output/interview/scorecard.json with fields: overall_score (0-100), rounds (array with at least 2 rounds each having name and score), strengths (array), improvements (array with 3+ items). Also write a follow-up thank you email draft to output/interview/thank_you_email.md.

5) Log self-improvement entries from this session: at least one learning, one error (you may simulate a command failure context), and one feature request. Use a structured format with IDs like [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX], [ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX], [FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX]. Save to output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md, output/.learnings/ERRORS.md, and output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md.

Follow privacy best practices: classify sensitive items (e.g., credentials) as secret and ensure they never appear in the recall summary. Use only relative paths in this workspace and write all outputs under output/.
